content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. Here, you only need to compute f(S)f(S)f(S) for the given binary string SSS.**

The beauty of a **binary** string TTT, denoted f(T)f(T)f(T), is defined as follows.

Consider the following algorithm (TTT is 111-indexed).


Let score=0\text{score} = 0score=0.
Repeat the following ∣T∣−1|T|-1∣T∣−1 times:

Choose an index iii (1≤i≤∣T∣1 \leq i \leq |T|1≤i≤∣T∣), delete TiT_iTi​ from the string, and concatenate the remaining parts together.

Note that this reduces the length of TTT by 111.
After the deletion, let KKK denote the number of indices iii such that 1≤i<∣T∣1 \leq i \lt |T|1≤i<∣T∣ and Ti≠Ti+1T_i \neq T_{i+1}Ti​=Ti+1​.
Add KKK to score\text{score}score.


The beauty of TTT is then defined to be the maximum possible value of score\text{score}score at the end of this algorithm.

You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN. Find f(S)f(S)f(S).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the length of SSS.
The next line contains the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line f(S)f(S)f(S).
constraints: SSS is a binary string, i.e, contains only the characters 000 and 111.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the following sequence:

Choose i=1i=1i=1. After this TTT becomes 010101 and KKK is equal to 111.
Add KKK to score\text{score}score, to obtain score=1\text{score} = 1score=1.
Choose i=1i=1i=1. After this TTT becomes 111 and KKK is equal to 000.
score\text{score}score remains 111.

It can be shown that no matter the sequence of operations, attaining score>1\text{score}\gt 1score>1 is not possible.
Test case 222: As all the characters of SSS are the same, the maximum possible score after the algorithm will be 000.
Test case 444: Consider the following sequence:

Choose i=2i=2i=2. After this TTT becomes 101110111011 and KKK is equal to 222. Our score is 222.
Choose i=4i=4i=4. After this TTT becomes 101101101 and KKK is equal to 222. Our score is 444.
Choose i=3i=3i=3. After this TTT becomes 101010 and KKK is equal to 111. Our score is 555.
Choose i=2i=2i=2. After this TTT becomes 111 and KKK is equal to 000. Our score is 555.

It can be shown that 555 is the maximum score we can reach.
